articles.title: Target Corp. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
articles.summary: Target Corp. (TGT) shares fell by 1.99% on Tuesday, closing at $127.14, a day when the broader stock market, including the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average, also experienced declines. This performance placed Target shares 4.48% below their 52-week high of $133.10 reached on April 21st.

articles.title: Outgoing Target Corp. CEO compensation rose 7% in 2025 as sales slump continued
articles.summary: Former Target CEO Brian Cornell received a 7% pay raise in his final year, bringing his total compensation to $21.8 million. This compensation increase occurred despite a continuing sales slump for the company in 2025. Current CEO Michael Fiddelke also saw a significant raise, 42%, before taking the top executive position.

articles.title: Target Corp. stock (US87612E1064): Is its everyday essentials edge strong enough to unlock new upside?
articles.summary: This report examines whether Target's core strength in everyday essentials and its focus on value can drive sustainable growth amid evolving consumer demands and intensifying competition. It delves into Target's business model, competitive landscape, and key risks to help investors assess its potential. The article highlights Target's stability, competitive positioning through private labels and digital integration, and analyst consensus on its value and growth potential.
